# Quick Fix: Dual Keyboard Issue on Password Fields
Generated: 20260218T125203Z

## Change Made

### 1. `app_text_field.dart`
- Added `isActive` parameter (default `false`) to constructor and field list
- Updated `enabledBorder` logic: when `isActive` is true, uses `focusedBorderColor` (or default) with width 2

### 2. `login_screen.dart`
- Removed `_passwordFocusNode` declaration, dispose call
- Password `AppTextField`: replaced `focusNode` + `requestFocus` with `isActive: _showKeypad`
- `onTap`: changed to `FocusScope.of(context).unfocus()` + setState
- `_hideKeypad`: replaced `_passwordFocusNode.unfocus()` with `FocusScope.of(context).unfocus()`

### 3. `register_screen.dart`
- Removed `_passwordFocusNode` and `_confirmFocusNode` declarations and dispose calls
- Password `AppTextField`: replaced `focusNode` + `requestFocus` with `isActive: _activeField == _ActiveField.password`
- Confirm `AppTextField`: replaced `focusNode` + `requestFocus` with `isActive: _activeField == _ActiveField.confirm`
- `_onTextFieldFocus`: removed `_passwordFocusNode.unfocus()` and `_confirmFocusNode.unfocus()` calls
- `_hideKeypad`: replaced unfocus calls with `FocusScope.of(context).unfocus()`

## Verification
- Pattern followed: Clean Architecture, existing widget reuse
- Root cause addressed: no `requestFocus()` on password fields means no input connection opened, so system keyboard is never triggered

## Files Modified
1. `/Users/osy/Desktop/projects/workCheck/attendance_app/lib/presentation/common_widgets/app_text_field.dart` - added `isActive` param and updated enabledBorder logic
2. `/Users/osy/Desktop/projects/workCheck/attendance_app/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/login_screen.dart` - removed passwordFocusNode, use isActive
3. `/Users/osy/Desktop/projects/workCheck/attendance_app/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/register_screen.dart` - removed passwordFocusNode/confirmFocusNode, use isActive

## Notes
No follow-up needed. The fix is self-contained and no other screens reference these focus nodes.
